prompt: A PQS is preparing a BOQ for a JCT Standard Building Contract using NRM2. Which document governs measurement rules?

options.text: NRM2: Detailed measurement for building works, with SMM7 legacy references only where contract specifies
CESMM4 without modification for building works
Contractor's internal pricing spreadsheet only
RICS Red Book valuation rules for all BOQ items

explanation: UK building BOQs for tender typically follow NRM2 measurement rules.

distractorExplanations.b: CESMM applies to civil engineering, not building BOQ under NRM2.
distractorExplanations.c: BOQ measurement must follow agreed standard rules for tender comparability.
distractorExplanations.d: Red Book governs valuation disputes, not tender BOQ preparation.

prompt: An item description reads 'Excavate foundation trenches, max depth 1.2m'. The site requires 2.1m deep trenches in rock. How should quantification treat this?

options.text: Measure all excavation to 1.2m rate regardless of actual depth
Amend description and measure additional depth/rock as separate items or provisional sum per tender strategy
Omit excavation because it is too complex
Hide rock excavation in preliminaries without measurement

explanation: BOQ preparation must align descriptions and quantities with site and design information.

distractorExplanations.a: BOQ must reflect anticipated scope or risk allowances.
distractorExplanations.c: Complex conditions need measured or provisional items, not omission.
distractorExplanations.d: Major scope items should be identifiable in BOQ or defined provisional sums.

prompt: Under NRM2, hollowcore precast concrete floor slabs should be measured in:

options.text: Linear metres along building perimeter only
Number of trucks delivered
Square metres stating thickness, noting structural screed and topping separately where applicable
Tonnes of concrete only without area

explanation: NRM2 sets unit conventions for precast flooring and associated notes.

distractorExplanations.a: Floor slabs are area-measured, not perimeter-linear.
distractorExplanations.b: BOQ measurement uses construction units per NRM2, not logistics units.
distractorExplanations.d: Precast slabs are typically measured in m² with thickness stated.

prompt: A contractor's estimator finds the BOQ concrete quantity is 15% below their take-off. What is the professional quantification response?

options.text: Price the BOQ quantity without review to save time
Automatically add 15% to every BOQ line
Ignore take-off because BOQ is always wrong
Reconcile differences against drawings, NRM2 rules, and query ambiguities before pricing

explanation: Estimators reconcile BOQ to take-off and raise queries on material variances.

distractorExplanations.a: Pricing should follow reconciled quantities or qualified assumptions.
distractorExplanations.b: Global uplifts without analysis distort tender comparability.

prompt: NRM1 is requested for an employer's pre-tender elemental cost plan. NRM2 BOQ will follow for tender. What is the relationship?

options.text: NRM1 structures cost planning elements; NRM2 provides detailed measurement for pricing and tender BOQ
NRM1 replaces NRM2 entirely on all projects
NRM2 is only for post-contract final accounts
They are identical documents with different names

explanation: NRM1 supports cost plans; NRM2 supports detailed measurement and BOQ.

distractorExplanations.b: NRM1 and NRM2 serve different purposes in the cost cycle.
distractorExplanations.c: NRM2 is used for tender BOQ and measurement during procurement.
distractorExplanations.d: NRM1 is elemental cost planning; NRM2 is detailed measurement.

prompt: A provisional sum for 'specialist façade access' is included in the BOQ. How should tenderers price it?

options.text: Distribute the PS value across unrelated items
Include only attendance/mark-up unless tender documents invite lump-sum pricing of the PS
Always include full £1m default in every tender
Exclude provisional sums from tender totals

explanation: Provisional sum pricing depends on tender instructions and contract re-measurement rules.

distractorExplanations.a: Provisional sums are re-measured or ascertained per contract terms.
distractorExplanations.c: PS pricing follows contract and tender instructions.
distractorExplanations.d: Tender totals typically include provisional sums as priced.

prompt: Prime cost sums for light fittings are in the employer's BOQ. What risk does the employer retain?

options.text: No risk — contractor guarantees PC sum final cost
All installation risk only with no supply risk
Difference between PC allowance and actual supply cost plus contractor attendance
Contractor carries supply and inflation risk always

explanation: PC sums allocate supply cost risk to the employer with contractor attendance.

distractorExplanations.a: PC sums transfer supply cost risk to employer subject to contract.
distractorExplanations.b: Employer typically carries supply price risk on PC items.
distractorExplanations.d: Unless fixed-price supply is stated, PC risk sits with employer.

prompt: When measuring stud partitions to NRM2, which factor is included in the measured area?

options.text: Only studs without boards because boards are finishing
Measure perimeter linear metres of rooms instead
Exclude all door openings regardless of size
Partition area measured face to face including openings unless rules state deduction thresholds

explanation: NRM2 partition measurement follows defined area rules and opening deductions.

distractorExplanations.a: Partition items include complete partition construction per NRM2.
distractorExplanations.b: Partitions are area-measured, not taken as room perimeter default.
distractorExplanations.c: NRM2 sets opening deduction rules; not all openings are excluded.

prompt: A BOQ for NEC Option B requires activity schedule instead of traditional BOQ. What quantification focus changes?

options.text: Price against defined activities/milestones with scope references rather than only trade bills
No measurement needed because NEC has no quantities
Use FIDIC Red Book BOQ format without change
Prepare Red Book valuation only

explanation: NEC Option B uses BOQ; quantification must align with chosen NEC option.

distractorExplanations.b: Option B uses bill of quantities; other options use different schedules.
distractorExplanations.c: Contract form dictates pricing document structure.
distractorExplanations.d: Tender pricing document must match NEC Option B requirements.

prompt: An apprentice QS measures external paving. The drawing shows 1,250 m² but site boundaries reduce workable area. What action is required?

options.text: Measure full drawing area ignoring boundaries
Adjust quantity to workable area per drawings and site constraints, note assumption in query if unclear
Use 1,000 m² as round number without basis
Leave paving unmeasured as provisional sum always

explanation: Measurement must reflect constructible scope with queries for ambiguities.

distractorExplanations.c: Rounded quantities need documented measurement basis.
distractorExplanations.d: Defined paving scope should be measured where information allows.

prompt: NRM3 is requested for maintenance quantification on a shopping centre. Its primary use is:

options.text: Tender BOQ for new stadium construction
Legal adjudication procedure guide
Order of cost estimates and elemental cost planning for building maintenance works
Structural engineering design calculations

explanation: NRM3 supports measurement and cost planning of maintenance works.

distractorExplanations.a: NRM3 addresses maintenance works, not new-build tender BOQ.
distractorExplanations.b: NRM3 is a measurement/cost standard, not dispute procedure.
distractorExplanations.d: NRM3 does not replace structural design standards.

prompt: A tender BOQ line for 'Formwork to foundations' is lumped with concrete supply and pour. What is the quantification concern?

options.text: Lumping is always preferred for speed
Formwork should never be measured
Only the total lump sum matters for employer
Combined items reduce tender transparency and comparability — split per NRM2 where possible

explanation: BOQ structure should separate major cost components per measurement rules.

distractorExplanations.a: Transparent BOQ structure supports competitive tender analysis.
distractorExplanations.b: Formwork is a measurable NRM2 item.
distractorExplanations.c: Employers need rate and quantity visibility for variations and valuations.

prompt: During post-contract measurement, a variation adds 14 nr extra doors. Contract uses NRM2 BOQ rates. How are they valued?

options.text: Value using contract rates for similar items or fair rates with quantity per variation rules
No measurement because variations are lump sum always
Use current market dayworks without contract check
Ignore door ironmongery as always included

explanation: Variation quantification follows contract BOQ rates and valuation clauses.

distractorExplanations.b: Variation valuation uses contract valuation rules and measured quantities.
distractorExplanations.d: Door variations must address ironmongery scope per contract.

prompt: A QS checks a subcontract BOQ for MEP services. Services drawings are schematic only. Best quantification approach?

options.text: Firm detailed quantities from schematic drawings without caveat
Use provisional quantities or provisional sums with defined design development and remeasurement mechanism
Omit MEP entirely from subcontract scope
Copy BOQ from a hospital project unchanged

explanation: Immature MEP design requires provisional quantities/sums and remeasurement provisions.

distractorExplanations.a: Immature design produces unreliable firm quantities.
distractorExplanations.c: MEP must be scoped with appropriate uncertainty treatment.
distractorExplanations.d: BOQs must reflect project-specific design and scope.

prompt: Bill No. 4 in a BOQ covers preliminaries. Which item belongs there under typical UK practice?

options.text: Structural steel permanent works
Client's land acquisition costs
Site management, temporary services, hoarding, and insurances specific to preliminaries
Architect's design fee

explanation: Preliminaries cover contractor site-specific setup and management costs.

distractorExplanations.a: Permanent works belong in measured works bills.
distractorExplanations.b: Land costs are outside contractor preliminaries.
distractorExplanations.d: Consultant fees are not contractor BOQ preliminaries.

prompt: An employer BOQ requires 'fixed quantity' items despite incomplete design. What tender risk arises?

options.text: No risk because contractors always absorb quantity errors
Tenderers ignore quantity and price lump sum only
Fixed quantities eliminate all variation entitlement
Tenderers may price risk into rates; employer may carry quantity error risk if design changes

explanation: Fixed quantities on incomplete design shift quantity risk and affect tender pricing.

distractorExplanations.a: Fixed quantities allocate quantity risk per contract and design completeness.
distractorExplanations.b: BOQ tenderers price stated quantities and rates.
distractorExplanations.c: Variations can still arise from scope or instruction changes.

prompt: Quantification of reinforcement should under NRM2 typically be:

options.text: Tonnes linked to structural schedules, noting bar type, diameter, and waste policy per BOQ
Counted as number of bars without weight
Included only inside concrete m³ rate always
Excluded because it is invisible after pour

explanation: NRM2 measures reinforcement in tonnes with specification linkage.

distractorExplanations.b: Reinforcement is weight-measured in tonnes per NRM2 practice.
distractorExplanations.c: Reinforcement is usually a separate measurable item.
distractorExplanations.d: Reinforcement is a major cost item requiring measurement.

prompt: A civil sub-package uses CESMM4 on a rail project. A building QS joins the team. What competency is required?

options.text: Force NRM2 on all rail track items regardless
Apply CESMM4 measurement rules and coordinate interfaces with building NRM2 packages
Avoid all measurement on civil packages
Use SMM7 for earthworks on rail

explanation: QS quantification may require CESMM4 on civil interfaces alongside NRM2 building works.

distractorExplanations.a: Civil works follow CESMM, not NRM2.
distractorExplanations.c: Interface coordination requires understanding both rule sets.
distractorExplanations.d: CESMM is the standard for civil engineering measurement.

prompt: Tender documents require electronic BOQ in CESMM/NRM compliant CSV for BIM take-off checking. Purpose is:

options.text: Replace the need for professional measurement entirely
Hide rates from the employer always
Enable consistent automated quantity extraction and audit trail against model and rules
Eliminate provisional sums by software default

explanation: Structured electronic BOQ supports BIM reconciliation and tender analysis.

distractorExplanations.a: BIM quantities still require rule-based validation by QS.
distractorExplanations.b: Electronic format supports transparency and analysis, not concealment.
distractorExplanations.d: Provisional items remain a professional judgment.

prompt: A measured item 'Brickwork in outer leaf' excludes insulation cavity. The insulation is:

options.text: Automatically included in brickwork rate
Ignored as architect's detail only
Added only in preliminaries
Measured separately as its own NRM2 item linked to wall build-up

explanation: Composite walls are split into measurable components per NRM2.

distractorExplanations.a: Cavity insulation is a separate measurable component.
distractorExplanations.b: Full wall build-up must be quantified for pricing.
distractorExplanations.c: Insulation is permanent works, not preliminaries.

prompt: Final account quantification dispute: contractor claims extra excavation; employer says BOQ covered it. First QS step?

options.text: Compare contract BOQ descriptions, drawings, site records, and variation/clause entitlement
Split difference 50/50 without analysis
Pay contractor claim in full to close account
Reject all claims automatically

explanation: Final account measurement disputes are resolved against contract documents and records.

distractorExplanations.b: Final account resolution requires contractual and measured evidence.
distractorExplanations.c: Claims must be substantiated against contract records.
distractorExplanations.d: Valid substantiated claims must be evaluated fairly.

prompt: For APC Level 3 quantification, which example best demonstrates competency?

options.text: Watching a senior QS measure without participating
Leading BOQ production, resolving measurement ambiguities, and pricing logic on a live project
Using only software output without review
Delegating all queries to the architect

explanation: Level 3 quantification is demonstrated through leadership of measurement and costing.

distractorExplanations.a: Level 3 requires personal technical leadership and advice.
distractorExplanations.c: Level 3 includes professional judgment on measurement.
distractorExplanations.d: QS leads quantification and contractual scope clarification.

prompt: A BOQ note states 'all quantities are provisional'. Under JCT SBC, tender comparison is affected because:

options.text: Provisional quantities make tender prices non-comparable always
Contractor must ignore quantities entirely
Tenderers may price differently for remeasurement risk; employer should analyse rate build-ups
Employer cannot award contract with provisional quantities

explanation: Provisional quantities require rate-focused tender analysis and remeasurement planning.

distractorExplanations.a: Comparable rate analysis remains possible with provisional quantities.
distractorExplanations.b: Tenderers still price BOQ items for comparison.
distractorExplanations.d: JCT contracts commonly use provisional quantities with remeasurement.

prompt: Pricing a BOQ item for 'Suspended ceiling — tile and grid' requires:

options.text: Guess a single figure from memory
Use architect's ceiling height as price only
Exclude grid system as supplied by others always
Rate build-up from material, labour, waste, access, and smallworks with measured m² quantity

explanation: BOQ pricing links measured quantities to substantiated rate build-ups.

distractorExplanations.a: Pricing requires structured build-up and quantity linkage.
distractorExplanations.b: Height affects components but pricing needs full build-up.
distractorExplanations.c: Scope must be confirmed; grid is typically included.

prompt: An international BOQ uses FIDIC conditions with method of measurement based on UK practice. Quantification should:

options.text: Follow contract-stated measurement method and coordinate with FIDIC re-measurement clauses
Apply only local QS habit with no contract check
Ignore BOQ because FIDIC is lump sum only always
Use JCT valuation rules in Dubai automatically

explanation: International contracts require adherence to stated measurement and FIDIC valuation rules.

distractorExplanations.b: Contract documents define applicable measurement rules.
distractorExplanations.c: FIDIC forms vary; many use BOQ or re-measurement.
distractorExplanations.d: Applicable law and contract determine measurement and valuation.
